prompt:
Design a Field Service Operations Hub to run an offline, on-premise service marketplace and workforce workflow for a multi-site facilities company. The user experience is delivered in a Dioxus web UI in English, optimized for dispatch desktops and shared kiosk tablets. Administrators configure a multi-level forum structure (zone → board) to host board rules and announcements, control board visibility (public to all employees vs. restricted to specific teams), and assign moderators whose permissions are scoped per board for post pinning, comment removal, and rule enforcement. Requesters browse a service catalog organized by hierarchical categories and tags, use full-text search with multi-filters such as price range (e.g., $50.00–$5,000.00), availability windows, rating, and an offline “distance” filter computed from locally stored site ZIP codes and service coverage radii in miles, then sort by “best rated,” “soonest available,” or “lowest price.” Users can favorite services and compare up to three side-by-side. After a work order is marked complete, requesters can submit a review within 14 days with a 1–5 star rating, text, selectable tags, and up to five JPG/PNG images (2 MB each), including one follow-up review; anti-spam limits cap reviews to three per user per day and strictly one review per order. For workforce development, interns submit internship plans and daily/weekly/monthly reports with rich text and attachments, and mentors comment and sign off; weekly reports are due Mondays at 12:00 PM local time and monthly reports by the 5th at 5:00 PM, with submissions up to 72 hours late automatically flagged and included in aggregate progress dashboards.

The backend uses Rocket to expose offline REST-style APIs over a local LAN (or single-machine deployment), with MySQL persisting all operational data and audit trails without any third-party dependencies. Authentication is local username/password only, requiring at least 12 characters and locking accounts for 15 minutes after five failed attempts; role-based access controls enforce Administrator, Moderator, Service Manager, Warehouse Manager, Mentor, Intern, and Requester permissions. Service search uses MySQL full-text indexing and deterministic ranking rules; review reputation is computed as a traceable score using a weighted average that decays over 180 days, with administrative display controls to pin exemplary reviews and collapse low-signal content. Multi-level warehouse management stores warehouse–zone–bin hierarchies, bin dimensions in inches, load limits in pounds, temperature zone attributes, and enable/disable status; every structural or capacity change writes a timestamped change record with the acting user. Full-lifecycle face data management supports capture/import for badge issuance and restricted-area access: images must meet minimum resolution (at least 320×320), acceptable brightness range, and low-blur thresholds; the operator must confirm a single, frontal face, and an optional short challenge (turn head left/right) is recorded for basic liveness checks. Face records are versioned, deduplicated using local perceptual fingerprints, can be deactivated without deletion, and retain a complete audit trail; all face media and attachments are stored locally with content hashes, and sensitive identifiers are encrypted at rest with field masking in the UI. Tamper-proof internship and review records are enforced via append-only event logs with hash chaining so edits remain traceable even when permitted by policy.
